data.title: Empire State Manufacturing Index turns positive in February, suprising to upside
data.description:
February Empire State Manufacturing Index: +5.7 vs. -0.5 consensus and -12.6 prior.

New Orders: 11.4 vs. -8.6 in January.

Shipments : 14.2 vs. -1.7 prior.

Price Paid: 11.1 vs. +29.1.

data.title: Revolve Renewable Power secures $1.5 million from project sale
data.description:
• Revolve Renewable Power (OTCQB:REVVF) on Tuesday announced the sale of a 3MW combined heat and power project from its distributed generation portfolio for $1.5 million.
  • The company said the proceeds from the sale will be reflected as revenues in its Q3 FY2025 financial results.
  • The combined heat and power generator unit (the "CHP Project") was originally acquired by Revolve in August 2022 as part of the $1.4m acquisition of Centrica Business Solutions Mexico.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Light & Wonder to acquire charitable gaming assets of Grover Gaming for $850M cash
data.description:
• Light & Wonder (NASDAQ:LNW) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire the charitable gaming assets of Grover Gaming and G2 Gaming (together, “Grover Gaming”) for $850M cash.
  • The deal also includes a four-year revenue-based earn-out of up to $200M in cash.
  • Grover Charitable Gaming has an installed base of over 10,000 leased electronic pull-tab units across five U.S. States, operating under a recurring revenue model.
  • In 2024 Grover Charitable Gaming generated revenue of $135M and Grover Adjusted EBITDA of $111M.
  • The company expects the transaction to close during Q2 2025.
  • Post acquisition, Light & Wonder will operate the business with a combination of Grover Gaming and Light & Wonder employees.
  • Light & Wonder expects to fund the acquisition with existing cash and incremental debt financing.
  • Post-acquisition, Light & Wonder expects its net debt leverage ratio on a combined basis to remain within its target range of 2.5x-3.5x.
  • Light & Wonder reaffirms its 2025 $1.4B consolidated AEBITDA target underpinned by continued strong organic growth across our existing businesses, and without any AEBITDA contribution from the acquisition of Grover Charitable Gaming.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Huawei launches tri-foldable phone Mate XT globally - report
data.description:
On Tuesday, Chinese tech giant Huawei held a global launch for the industry's first tri-foldable phone in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, the Associated Press reported.

The company's Mate XT, which was first launched in China in September last year, will have a price tag of €3,499 (about $3,660), the report added.

Huawei did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Mate XT, a Z-shaped phone and three panels, saw over 3 million pre-orders on the company's Vmall website in September 2024.

Huawei was once in competition with Apple (AAPL) and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) to be the world's biggest handset maker until U.S. restrictions, starting in 2019, began to curb its access to chip manufacturing tools needed to produce its most advanced models.

However, in the past two years, Huawei has been making a comeback. In August 2023, the company surprised many by quietly launching its new flagship smartphone, Mate 60 Pro. Last year, the company launched its flagship Mate 70 smartphone, Mate XT, and the Pura 70 series.

Huawei and other Chinese smartphone makers are giving a tough challenge to Apple in China. Apple slipped to third spot in the Chinese market with a 17.1% market share, behind Huawei with an 18.1% share and second-placed Xiaomi with 17.2%, in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to Counterpoint Research.

However, Apple is also working on a foldable iPhone, which is currently in the planning stage.

data.title: Constellation Energy raises dividend by 10% to $0.3878
data.description:
• Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) declares $0.3878/share quarterly dividend, 10% increase from prior dividend of $0.3525.
  • Forward yield 0.51%
  • Payable March 18; for shareholders of record March 7; ex-div March 7.
  • See CEG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Exco Technologies announces normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Exco Technologies (TSX:XTC:CA) announced that the Toronto Stock Exchange has approved the company's normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the normal course issuer bid, Exco has the right to purchase for cancellation, from February 20, 2025 to February 19, 2026, a maximum of 1.77M common shares.
  • As of February 12, 2025, Exco had 38.5M shares of common shares issued and outstanding.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Fiera Capital invests $100M seed fund in OAKS EM Select strategy
data.description:
• Fiera Capital (TSX:FSZ:CA) on Tuesday announced a $100M seed investment into the Oaks EM Select strategy in the U.S. in a privately offered fund.
  • The strategy, led by Stefan Böttcher and Dominic Bokor-Ingram, focuses on identifying high-potential, under-researched securities in key emerging markets such as Saudi Arabia, Greece, Vietnam, Mexico, Indonesia, and the Philippines.
  • Fiera believes that this innovative approach addresses growing investor demand for diversification beyond the traditional "Big Six" emerging markets—China, Taiwan, Korea, India, Brazil, and South Africa—by targeting regions with robust growth dynamics that are often overlooked by mainstream investors.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Great Lakes Dredge & Dock GAAP EPS of $0.29 beats by $0.06, revenue of $202.77M misses by $8.43M
data.description:
• Great Lakes Dredge & Dock press release (NASDAQ:GLDD): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.29 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $202.77M (+11.6% Y/Y) misses by $8.43M.
  • Fourth quarter Adjusted EBITDA of $40.2 million.
  • Dredging backlog of $1.2 billion at December 31, 2024.
  • At December 31, 2024, the company had $10.2 million in cash and cash equivalents and total long-term debt of $448.2 million including $35 million outstanding against our $300 million revolver.
  • Total capital expenditures for 2024 were $135.7 million compared to $144.8 million for 2023.

data.title: Light & Wonder strikes deal to enter the charitable gaming category
data.description:
Light & Wonder (NASDAQ:LNW) announced on Tuesday that it acquired the charitable gaming assets of Grover Gaming and G2 Gaming for a total consideration of $850 million cash.

The assets held by the Grover Charitable Gaming were noted to have an installed base of over 10,000 leased electronic pull-tab units across five U.S. States (North Dakota, Ohio, Virginia, Kentucky, and New Hampshire), operating under a recurring revenue model. The business was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Greenville, North Carolina. Grover Charitable Gaming is seen as having a leading position in the fast-growing charitable gaming space and a best-in-class supplier in every state it operates across.

For the year ended December 31, on an unaudited and preliminary basis, Grover Charitable Gaming generated revenue of $135 million and adjusted EBITDA of $111 million.

The deal is subject to customary purchase price adjustments, and a four-year revenue based earn-out of up to $200 million cash. Upfront consideration implies a multiple of ~7.7x based on 2024 Grover Adjusted EBITDA2 and a ~7.1x multiple based on Grover run rate adjusted EBITDA. The company expects the transaction to close during the second quarter of 2025, subject to required regulatory and other approvals and customary closing conditions.

The transaction complements Light & Wonder's (NASDAQ:LNW) cross-platform strategy, by expanding into another high-growth, regulated adjacency in which the company is not currently a participant.

"Grover Gaming is a leading player in charitable gaming, a category that has experienced significant growth in recent years," highlighted CEO Matt Wilson. "This transaction complements our position as the leading cross-platform global games company by adding another compelling regulated adjacency to our profile," he added.

Light & Wonder (LNW) expects to fund the acquisition with existing cash and incremental debt financing. Post-acquisition, Light & Wonder expects its net debt leverage ratio4 on a combined basis to remain within its target range of 2.5X to 3.5X.

data.title: The AZEK company acquires Northwest Polymers
data.description:
• The AZEK Company (NYSE:AZEK) said on Tuesday that it has acquired Northwest Polymers, expanding its capacity to source and process recycled materials.
  • “Located in close proximity to our Boise manufacturing facility, this acquisition will help us strengthen our material supply chain in the western United States, improve cost efficiencies, and enhance our ability to meet the growing demand for beautiful, low-maintenance and sustainable outdoor living solutions,” said Jesse Singh, CEO of The AZEK Company.
  • Source: Press Release
